intent: KM directive from in-Atrium voice review 2026-06-03: a full book review must NOT produce ~100 approvable cards. ONE session = ONE packet. Agents read the whole page-tagged transcript, ASSESS CONTEXT incl. whether a note applies to multiple books, GROUP into a few meaningful edits, and UPDATE THE BOOK IN THE BACKGROUND. KM does NOT approve every change (his voice feedback is the disposition). Only big/cross-cutting/material calls escalate back as a SINGLE card. Page tags are approximate hints (30s granularity), not precise. Err toward fewer cards.

lgp.families_first_impact: minimal human burden — KM stays at feedback, agents carry implementation; the 4-hour-workweek principle applied to book review

next_gate: GB prep + Tiger implementation honor this when turning sessions into work; surfaces only meaningful/escalated cards

intent: KM clarification 2026-06-03: book->code is not only the harness series — Programmable ERP, AND even private books, all drive code. Whether an edit materializes in PUBLIC code or PRIVATE repos is the operator choice. Federation intent: enable each sovereign user to have their own public/private code + enable their agents to share/resonate in some way (receipted-shard propagation — share capabilities/patterns, not private content unless chosen). Implication for the book->code drilldown (Step D): public/private is an operator setting per edit/repo; the loop is the same.

lgp.families_first_impact: sovereign code ownership per operator; agents amplify without surrendering private IP — LGP federation

next_gate: fold into Meta-Vision doc (Tiger) + reflect public/private in Step D book->code drilldown design
title: Vision: book->code is UNIVERSAL (all series + private books); federation = sovereign public/private code + agent sharing

intent: KM clarification: (1) CO-EXTRUSION — when a book initial manuscript is written, its initial code + unit tests + integration tests are written at the SAME time. (2) WORKFLOW.md gains a TECHNICAL/ARCHITECTURAL REVIEW BOARD alongside the editorial board, so human-handoff arrives with fully-specd, tested, functional code already in place. (3) KM REVIEW = accept original code + diffs: Atrium shows the DIFFS (his feedback -> changes to already-developed code/manuscript) QUICK, BEFORE they are written; his acceptance covers original code AND diffs. HARD CONSTRAINT: grouped to a handful, NEVER ~50 approvals (an hour of review cannot become 50 gates). Step C v1 = diff-review-in-Atrium (grouped, see-before-write, reversible, sealed), NOT auto-apply, NOT per-edit. For prose-only books today (B11/B12) the diffs are manuscript diffs; code diffs appear once co-extrusion exists.

lgp.families_first_impact: human stays at the stillpoint reviewing meaning, not drowning in approvals; code always reflects the book (alignment)

next_gate: GB: add Technical/Architectural Review Board to WORKFLOW.md (canon). Tiger: build Atrium diff-review surface (grouped, see-before-write). KM ratifies.
title: Pipeline + Step C shape (KM 2026-06-03): co-extrude book+code+tests at authoring; KM reviews via grouped diffs (see-before-write, few approvals)

intent: KM meta insight: do we have the Atrium defined in book/NLP format? Finding: YES — Series 2 Vol 2 The Primacy Cockpit IS the Atrium book. BUT todays Atrium (Book Review surface, ATR-3/4/5b/6, Dragon-Whisper session recorder, diff-review/Step C, loopback-trust live node, drilldown) was built CODE-FIRST from KMs voice/chat — it has outpaced/diverged from Vol 2. This violates KMs OWN book<->code coherence rule: the alignment MECHANISM (book) must govern the alignment SURFACE (Atrium) itself. Self-referential closure. ACTION: reconcile the Atriums NLP spec back into Vol 2 (living spec) + apply GBs prose<->code coherence check to the Atrium<->Vol2 pairing; govern future Atrium changes book-first (code edits also reflect back to the book). Also reframes the diff-review: it is a UNIVERSAL change-review surface — book->code and direct-code-edit are two SOURCES into the same accept-before-write+seal pipeline (provenance differs: book passage vs direct observation).

lgp.families_first_impact: the alignment tool is itself aligned — no un-booked code in the cockpit; self-consistent system humans can trust

next_gate: GB: plan Atrium<->Vol2 reconciliation (canon). Tiger: reflect the ATR-* code into Vol2 living spec. KM ratifies.
ref: Series 2 Vol 2 The Primacy Cockpit / Atrium
title: Atrium↔book coherence (KM meta 2026-06-03): the alignment SURFACE has drifted code-first from its book (Vol 2 Primacy Cockpit)

intent: Tiger assessment of GB artifact Tiger_Path_Forward_Book_Defined_UX_Minimal_Human_Loop_2026-06-04. Direction ~85% right + ADOPTED: design-state-model-first → high-level ratify → build once + reviewer pass + batch; minimal human = Accept only; no realtime debug; book(HMC voice + manuscript) defines UX; GB stays meta; G lightweight guardrail. KM-RATIFIED 4 STEERS: (1) HOLD the new Hopper-lane build — stabilize + validate one clean real Step-C session start-to-finish FIRST; adding a surface mid-stabilization is the scope-creep G warned against; Hopper is a next, not now. (2) RIGHT-SIZE: design-note+ratify for SUBSTANTIAL new surfaces; small fixes/polish stay lightweight (Tiger self-review + batch). (3) SPLIT the design note: GB derives the EXPERIENCE from voice; TIGER owns the technical STATE MODEL + reviewer pass (interaction-state bugs live in transitions/poll guards, not voice — GB cannot spec those from voice). (4) PRACTICE before over-canonizing: discipline is right but run it on ONE real cycle before locking WORKFLOW 17.5/17.6 deep. Privacy: KM consents to GB live-unsealed-HMC read access (his data/agent; will adjust if issue). Channel: the coordination ledger IS the Tiger↔G channel; GB reads it.

lgp.families_first_impact: discipline upgrade kills whack-a-mole UX churn; human stays at stillpoint (ratify+Accept only); stabilize-before-expand protects the working loop

next_gate: GB applies the 4 steers (hold Hopper build; right-size; split design note ownership; do not over-canonize). KM validates a real Step-C session next. Tiger adopts design-then-build for substantial UX.
title: GB path-forward (Book-Defined UX / minimal-human) — APPROVED w/ 4 steers (KM ratified)
